key_insight: A reduced-integration virtual element formulation with scaled boundary parametrization can cut quadrature to one point per section while still capturing finite-strain nonlinear solid response reasonably well.
engineering_relevance: This offers a potentially efficient stabilization strategy for complex deformation analyses in computational solid mechanics, especially when standard integration is costly or difficult on irregular elements.
method: Expand constitutive quantities in a Taylor series about the sectional center.
Integrate the weak form analytically using a scaled boundary parametrization.
Use only one integration point per section for the reduced-integration VEM formulation.
evidence_signal: The authors validated the method on several numerical examples, including a nonlinear patch test and large-deformation cases with hyperelastic anisotropy and elasto-plasticity, and compared it against Q2 and Q1STc+ elements.
watch_item: The patch test was not satisfied for higher-order shape functions, so accuracy may depend on element geometry and how closely the physical element matches the assumed parent element.
paper_title: Reduced integration with scaled boundary parametrization for virtual elements at finite strains
